cases.question: List all departments alphabetically.
cases.expected_sql: SELECT name AS department FROM departments ORDER BY name

cases.question: How many departments are there?
cases.expected_sql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS department_count FROM departments

cases.question: Show every employee in alphabetical order.
cases.expected_sql: SELECT name AS employee FROM employees ORDER BY name

cases.question: What is the employee count?
cases.expected_sql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS employee_count FROM employees

cases.question: Who has the highest annual salary?
cases.expected_sql: SELECT name AS employee, salary FROM employees ORDER BY salary DESC, name LIMIT 1

cases.question: Who has the lowest annual salary?
cases.expected_sql: SELECT name AS employee, salary FROM employees ORDER BY salary ASC, name LIMIT 1

cases.question: What is the average salary across all employees?
cases.expected_sql: SELECT ROUND(AVG(salary), 2) AS average_salary FROM employees

cases.question: What is the total annual payroll?
cases.expected_sql: SELECT SUM(salary) AS total_salary FROM employees

cases.question: How many employees earn more than 100000 euros?
cases.expected_sql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS employees_above_100k FROM employees WHERE salary > 100000

cases.question: List Engineering employees from highest to lowest salary.
cases.expected_sql: SELECT e.name AS employee, e.salary FROM employees e JOIN departments d ON d.id = e.department_id WHERE d.name = 'Engineering' ORDER BY e.salary DESC, e.name

cases.question: Who earns the most in Engineering?
cases.expected_sql: SELECT e.name AS employee, e.salary FROM employees e JOIN departments d ON d.id = e.department_id WHERE d.name = 'Engineering' ORDER BY e.salary DESC, e.name LIMIT 1

cases.question: What is the average salary in Engineering?
cases.expected_sql: SELECT ROUND(AVG(e.salary), 2) AS average_salary FROM employees e JOIN departments d ON d.id = e.department_id WHERE d.name = 'Engineering'

cases.question: Show the employee count for each department.
cases.expected_sql: SELECT d.name AS department, COUNT(e.id) AS employee_count FROM departments d LEFT JOIN employees e ON e.department_id = d.id GROUP BY d.id, d.name ORDER BY d.name

cases.question: Show average salary by department from highest to lowest.
cases.expected_sql: SELECT d.name AS department, ROUND(AVG(e.salary), 2) AS average_salary FROM departments d JOIN employees e ON e.department_id = d.id GROUP BY d.id, d.name ORDER BY average_salary DESC, d.name

cases.question: Show total payroll by department from highest to lowest.
cases.expected_sql: SELECT d.name AS department, SUM(e.salary) AS total_salary FROM departments d JOIN employees e ON e.department_id = d.id GROUP BY d.id, d.name ORDER BY total_salary DESC, d.name

cases.question: Which departments have an average salary above 90000 euros?
cases.expected_sql: SELECT d.name AS department, ROUND(AVG(e.salary), 2) AS average_salary FROM departments d JOIN employees e ON e.department_id = d.id GROUP BY d.id, d.name HAVING AVG(e.salary) > 90000 ORDER BY average_salary DESC, d.name

cases.question: Who is the highest paid employee in each department?
cases.expected_sql: WITH ranked AS (SELECT d.name AS department, e.name AS employee, e.salary, ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY d.id ORDER BY e.salary DESC, e.name) AS salary_rank FROM departments d JOIN employees e ON e.department_id = d.id) SELECT department, employee, salary FROM ranked WHERE salary_rank = 1 ORDER BY department

cases.question: Which employees have no department?
cases.expected_sql: SELECT name AS employee FROM employees WHERE department_id IS NULL ORDER BY name

cases.question: List employees earning at least 95000 euros from highest to lowest.
cases.expected_sql: SELECT name AS employee, salary FROM employees WHERE salary >= 95000 ORDER BY salary DESC, name

cases.question: What is the total payroll for Sales?
cases.expected_sql: SELECT SUM(e.salary) AS total_salary FROM employees e JOIN departments d ON d.id = e.department_id WHERE d.name = 'Sales'

cases.question: Zeige alle Projekte absteigend nach Budget.
cases.expected_sql: SELECT name AS project, budget FROM projects ORDER BY budget DESC, name

cases.question: Welches Projekt hat das höchste Budget?
cases.expected_sql: SELECT name AS project, budget FROM projects ORDER BY budget DESC, name LIMIT 1

cases.question: Welches Projekt hat das niedrigste Budget?
cases.expected_sql: SELECT name AS project, budget FROM projects ORDER BY budget ASC, name LIMIT 1

cases.question: Wie hoch ist das Gesamtbudget aller Projekte?
cases.expected_sql: SELECT SUM(budget) AS total_project_budget FROM projects

cases.question: Wie hoch ist das durchschnittliche Projektbudget?
cases.expected_sql: SELECT ROUND(AVG(budget), 2) AS average_project_budget FROM projects

cases.question: Welche Projekte sind aktiv?
cases.expected_sql: SELECT name AS project FROM projects WHERE status = 'active' ORDER BY name

cases.question: Wie viele aktive Projekte gibt es?
cases.expected_sql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS active_project_count FROM projects WHERE status = 'active'

cases.question: Wie hoch ist das Gesamtbudget der aktiven Projekte?
cases.expected_sql: SELECT SUM(budget) AS active_project_budget FROM projects WHERE status = 'active'

cases.question: Zeige die Anzahl der Projekte pro Status.
cases.expected_sql: SELECT status, COUNT(*) AS project_count FROM projects GROUP BY status ORDER BY status

cases.question: Zeige das Projektbudget pro Status absteigend.
cases.expected_sql: SELECT status, SUM(budget) AS total_budget FROM projects GROUP BY status ORDER BY total_budget DESC, status

cases.question: Welche Projekte gehören zur Abteilung Engineering?
cases.expected_sql: SELECT p.name AS project, p.budget FROM projects p JOIN departments d ON d.id = p.department_id WHERE d.name = 'Engineering' ORDER BY p.budget DESC, p.name

cases.question: Zeige das gesamte Projektbudget pro Abteilung absteigend.
cases.expected_sql: SELECT d.name AS department, SUM(p.budget) AS total_budget FROM departments d JOIN projects p ON p.department_id = d.id GROUP BY d.id, d.name ORDER BY total_budget DESC, d.name

cases.question: Welche Abteilung hat insgesamt das höchste Projektbudget?
cases.expected_sql: SELECT d.name AS department, SUM(p.budget) AS total_budget FROM departments d JOIN projects p ON p.department_id = d.id GROUP BY d.id, d.name ORDER BY total_budget DESC, d.name LIMIT 1

cases.question: Welche Projekte enden im Jahr 2026?
cases.expected_sql: SELECT name AS project, end_date FROM projects WHERE end_date >= DATE '2026-01-01' AND end_date < DATE '2027-01-01' ORDER BY end_date, name

cases.question: Welche Projekte starten im Jahr 2026?
cases.expected_sql: SELECT name AS project, start_date FROM projects WHERE start_date >= DATE '2026-01-01' AND start_date < DATE '2027-01-01' ORDER BY start_date, name

cases.question: Liste alle abgeschlossenen Projekte alphabetisch auf.
cases.expected_sql: SELECT name AS project FROM projects WHERE status = 'completed' ORDER BY name

cases.question: Welche Projekte sind geplant?
cases.expected_sql: SELECT name AS project FROM projects WHERE status = 'planned' ORDER BY name

cases.question: Welche Abteilungen haben aktive Projekte?
cases.expected_sql: SELECT DISTINCT d.name AS department FROM departments d JOIN projects p ON p.department_id = d.id WHERE p.status = 'active' ORDER BY d.name

cases.question: Wie viele Projekte hat jede Abteilung?
cases.expected_sql: SELECT d.name AS department, COUNT(p.id) AS project_count FROM departments d LEFT JOIN projects p ON p.department_id = d.id GROUP BY d.id, d.name ORDER BY d.name

cases.question: Zeige die Anzahl der Mitarbeiter und Projekte pro Abteilung.
cases.expected_sql: SELECT d.name AS department, COUNT(DISTINCT e.id) AS employee_count, COUNT(DISTINCT p.id) AS project_count FROM departments d LEFT JOIN employees e ON e.department_id = d.id LEFT JOIN projects p ON p.department_id = d.id GROUP BY d.id, d.name ORDER BY d.name
